summary: Implemented the revision-2 Specialist Run foundation in exactly six authorized files. Added closed types and schema, exact constants and limits, SC4401-SC4405, defensive package-bound validation, canonical create and raw restore operations, and no transition or inspection behavior. Focused format, lint, typecheck, schema, lifecycle, adversarial, immutability, and byte-scope checks pass.

artifacts.name: foundation-implementation.md
artifacts.content:
# Foundation Implementation

## Outcome

Pass. Foundation GoalContract revision 2 was implemented without resuming revision 1, widening scope, changing Git state, using network access, or adding host effects.

## Files Changed

- `schemas/v1alpha1/specialist-run.schema.json`: closed session and inspection schema; 17405 bytes; SHA-256 `8949156f35ab5c4e9efcc86c5509b6a0fb137ced45a0c4c6da3648621797bba5`.
- `src/constants.ts`: exact Specialist Run API version, kinds, and limits; 2571 bytes; SHA-256 `b7050ac085a3fcd7dad839e3fbe930ecbf49224d40a37f0a44dd15052bc06058`.
- `src/diagnostics.ts`: SC4401-SC4405 definitions; 25190 bytes; SHA-256 `3d27fe071994a2a87a59ecadc52a788a6aaf85737b46371f5622bf10c50aede6`.
- `src/specialist-run-schema.ts`: strict AJV session, inspection, and root validators; 3371 bytes; SHA-256 `705357ee174691f4e5b177f1a9d4ff3abf38adad46ce32264f9dd3f846b5dd23`.
- `src/specialist-run-session.ts`: defensive validator plus create and raw restore operations; 18144 bytes; SHA-256 `e84765ebafafcbb89cdf5cb3645444be7a3f16d5857f4a531779e240deb43bcc`.
- `src/specialist-run-types.ts`: complete frozen session and inspection declarations; 4222 bytes; SHA-256 `01c54b2fc3244f875a4c04e8624d570953e08a6ce1cbbadb8e766117e270a74c`.

All final files are ASCII with LF line endings and contain no TAB, CR, or unsafe control bytes.

## Decisions

- Reused `verifySpecialistPackage` and `verifySpecialistHandoff` without reclassifying their V11 diagnostics.
- Registered the exact session digest domain `swecircuit/specialist-run/session/v1alpha1`; inspection declarations and schema are present, but no inspection implementation was added.
- Embedded the canonical package returned by V11 verification and reconstructed goal, compilation, package, selected-candidate, manifest, and blueprint bindings from it.
- Required canonical padded base64, exact raw byte count, raw SHA-256, verified handoff identity and outcome, ordinal agent ordering, uniqueness, transitive passing dependencies, and at most one non-pass row on restore.
- Bound compilation parsing to the existing V11 `SPECIALIST_LIMITS.outputBytes` ceiling so a valid package up to 4 MiB is not narrowed by the generic parser default.
- Mapped malformed closed-session and restore semantics to SC4401, aggregate or raw resource excess to SC4402, and retained SC4307-SC4313 and SC9001 from composed V11 operations.
- Kept provider, runtime, persistence, transition, inspection, Git, merge, and memory effects absent.

## Source Verification

The generated revision-2 contract was read first. A read-only PowerShell verifier then recomputed raw SHA-256 and byte count for all 22 declared repository sources. Every source matched its exact contract binding before any source content was relied upon.

## Fallback Protocol

Native `apply_patch` was attempted first and failed before reading or mutating `src/constants.ts` with the known sandbox refresh error `helper_unknown_error: setup refresh had errors`.

Before fallback mutation, `src/constants.ts` was asserted at SHA-256 `f00156e582cafaea19f6db6f55e042422c488b660483f5fccee1d6b1988cee6f`, `src/diagnostics.ts` at SHA-256 `570e2d2349f6e466999f7a8d9c15b0f82342afa4ccd1042729c282e66c4a7f14`, and the four new files were asserted nonexistent. Every fallback write used an exact UTF-8 payload or unique exact replacement after a current SHA-256 precondition, remained inside the six-file scope, and was immediately byte-compared and rehashed. Biome formatting was also guarded by the six current hashes before its escalated retry.

## Verification

- `npm.cmd exec -- biome format schemas/v1alpha1/specialist-run.schema.json src/constants.ts src/diagnostics.ts src/specialist-run-schema.ts src/specialist-run-session.ts src/specialist-run-types.ts` - PASS; checked six files with no fixes required.
- `npm.cmd exec -- biome lint src/constants.ts src/diagnostics.ts src/specialist-run-schema.ts src/specialist-run-session.ts src/specialist-run-types.ts` - PASS; checked five files with no fixes required.
- `npm.cmd run typecheck` - PASS; `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` exited 0.
- `node --input-type=module -e` strict AJV probe - PASS; root, session, and inspection schemas compiled under strict Draft 2020-12 settings.
- `node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e` lifecycle probe - PASS; a real one-agent V11 request compiled and rendered, creation passed, serialization and fresh restore passed, harmless outer whitespace and reordered root properties converged, and outputs were detached and deeply frozen.
- The same lifecycle probe verified SC4307 for wrong package and compilation expectations and SC4401 for unknown fields, stale bindings, duplicate keys, malformed UTF-8, and proxied raw bytes.
- A defensive validator probe verified that accessor and proxy session objects fail with SC4401 without invoking the accessor.
- A closed-surface probe verified exact constants, limits, SC4401-SC4405 severity and exit code, exact session, goal, and accepted-row keys, and absence of inspect or record implementations.
- Final PowerShell scope and byte audit - PASS; exactly the six declared outputs were accounted for and all were LF-only ASCII.

## Failed Attempts

- Native `apply_patch` failed before mutation because the Windows sandbox helper could not refresh.
- The first read-only line-ending probe had a PowerShell empty-pipe parser error; the corrected probe passed and made no mutation.
- The first fallback payload composer stopped before invoking PowerShell because the tool isolate lacked `TextEncoder`; no mutation occurred.
- The first guarded PowerShell writer had invalid generic `SequenceEqual[byte]` syntax and failed during parsing before mutation; byte comparison was replaced with exact base64 equality.
- The initial formatter check exited 1 because four files required mechanical formatting; no files were changed by that check.
- The first guarded formatter write was denied by the default sandbox and reported no fixes; the required escalated retry used the same hash preconditions and passed.
- The first inline AJV probe lost JavaScript quotes through PowerShell parsing; a shell-safe equivalent passed.
- The first inline lifecycle probe had a quoted duplicate-key fixture parse error before execution; a shell-safe fixture passed.

## Residual Risks

- Persistent V12 regression tests, package exports, inspection, record transitions, integrated review, and the canonical repository gate are outside this six-file foundation authority.
- This foundation proves create and restore semantics only. It intentionally makes no launch, scheduling, persistence, integration, merge, or memory claim.

## Follow-Ups

- Dependent work should implement inspection and record semantics against `validateSpecialistRunSession` without weakening package or raw-handoff verification.
- The integration owner should add authorized exports and focused persistent tests, then run the complete repository verification and independent review gates.

risks: Persistent V12 regression tests, package exports, inspection, record transitions, integrated review, and the full repository gate remain outside this six-file work unit.
The foundation intentionally provides no provider, runtime, persistence, launch, Git, merge, or memory effects.
followUps: Implement inspection and record semantics against the defensive session validator in their separately approved work units.
Add authorized exports and persistent tests during integration, then run the canonical repository gate and independent review.
